calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Modifying Administrative And Dispositive Provisions Of The Trust
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Cases PTR-16-299785 and PTR-16-299786 were heard together.
Appearances: Lauren Cappeloni and Darrin Ng for and with petitioner Peter Johnson.

The Court stated that the petitions raised several issues which needed to be addressed. First, he was concerned that the proposed revision to remove the restriction on distributions after age 25 upon failure to complete four years of college was not in accordance with the trustor's express intentions as stated in the trust document. Ms. Cappelloni responded that she did not believe that the trustor intended to prohibit interim payments after age 25, but to provide for an additional incentive payment for each beneficiary who satisfied the college attendance prerequisite. Mr. Jahnson added that while one of the beneficiaries was already attending college at USC and that the second could be expected to follow suit, the third beneficiary had a learning disability which might prevent him from being able to complete four yeard of college. He was sure that it would not have been the intent of the trustor to cut this beneficiary off from trust income for 15 years as a result of the learning disability, but that providing an incentive for an additional or bonus payment for a collelge education would be consistent with the trustor's actual intent.
The Court next addressed the qustion of whether the final payment was to be made at age 35 or 40. The Court stated his opinion that while two provisions of the trust document were in conflict, the proposed revision to age 40 was more consistent with the other provisions of the trust document. Ms. Cappeloni stated that this was the conclusion the petitioners had reached as well.
The Court then addressed the designated corporate trustee. He stated that the problem here was the failure of the trustee to comply with the trust designation of Wells Fargo Bank, but selecting instead Northern Trust. Mr. Johnson explained that the trustor had little ongoing relationship with Wells Fargo, and that when he had interviewed several banks and what they could do for the trust, he had been most impressed by the service Northern Trust could provide and believed its selection as corporate trustee was in the best interests of the trust.
Lastly, the Court was troubled by the request to authorize Mr. Johnson to select the successor trustee, as he noted thts would be an extension of the trustee's powers not granted by the trust instrument.
The Court stated that he was leaning towards granting the clarification of age 40 as being the age for the final distribution and allowing the substitution of Northern Trust in place of Wells Fargo Bank, but not the allowance of the other proposed modifications absent a showing of changed circumstances. He agreed to study the matter further before making his final decision. The matters were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)
